title.en: Ensure chrony is running as user _chrony

prose.en: Ensure chrony is running as user _chrony

description.en: The `chrony` package is installed with a dedicated user account `_chrony`. This account is granted the access required by the `chronyd` service

rationale.en: The `chronyd` service should run with only the required privlidges

audit.en:
Add or edit the `user` line to `/etc/chrony/chrony.conf` or a file ending in `.conf` in `/etc/chrony/conf.d/`:  

**\- OR \-**  

If another time synchronization service is in use on the system, run the following command to remove `chrony` from the system:

```
# apt purge chrony
# apt autoremove chrony
```

```
user _chrony
```

remediation.en:
Add or edit the `user` line to `/etc/chrony/chrony.conf` or a file ending in `.conf` in `/etc/chrony/conf.d/`:  

**\- OR \-**  

If another time synchronization service is in use on the system, run the following command to remove `chrony` from the system:

```
# apt purge chrony
# apt autoremove chrony
```

```
user _chrony
```
